_readme: Quote of the Day corpus — the SOURCE file (design spec §3). Edit HERE,
then run `npm run quotes` to regenerate public/data/quotes.json and
re-seal the data manifest. Never hand-edit the emitted file.
Sourcing follows the spec's green list (§3.3): public-domain translations
only. The build script enforces the red list. Per the corpus workflow
(§3.4), entries are drafted with their full locus and marked
verified:false until checked against a printed edition of the named
translation; the harness counts both states so the verification debt is

quotes.text: Thou madest us for Thyself, and our heart is restless, until it repose in Thee.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. E. B. Pusey (1838), public domain

quotes.text: Too late loved I Thee, O Thou Beauty of ancient days, yet ever new! too late I loved Thee!
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. E. B. Pusey (1838), public domain

quotes.text: This is the Halleluia which we sing, which, as you know, meaneth, Praise ye the Lord. Therefore that period is before the Lord's Resurrection, this, after His Resurrection: by which time is signified the future hope which as yet we have not: for what we represent after the Lord's Resurrection, we shall have after our own.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series I, vol. 8 (Schaff ed.), public domain

quotes.text: But our very Life descended hither, and bore our death, and slew it, out of the abundance of His own life.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. J. G. Pilkington, NPNF series I, vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: Grace does not destroy nature, but perfects it.
quotes.author: St. Thomas Aquinas
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Fathers of the English Dominican Province (1911–1925), public domain

quotes.text: In the heart of the Church, my Mother, I will be Love.
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. T. N. Taylor (1912), public domain

quotes.text: All true and living devotion presupposes the love of God; and indeed it is neither more nor less than a very real love of God.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: 19th-century English translation (Rivingtons, 1876), public domain

quotes.text: Would you do honour to Christ's body? Neglect Him not when naked; do not while here you honour Him with silken garments, neglect Him perishing without of cold and nakedness.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series I, vol. 10, public domain

quotes.text: Do you fast? Give me proof of it by your works. If you see a poor man, take pity on him.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series I, vol. 9, public domain

quotes.text: Hell took a body, and met God face to face. It took earth, and encountered Heaven.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: traditional translation, public domain

quotes.text: Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.
quotes.author: St. Jerome
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Commentary on Isaiah, prologue (PL 24); the common English rendering of "scripturarum ignoratio, Christi ignoratio est" (cf. CCC 133)

quotes.text: The divine words grow with the one who reads them.
quotes.author: St. Gregory the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Homilies on Ezekiel I, hom. 7, 8 (PL 76, 843D); rendering of the Latin "Divina eloquia cum legente crescunt"

quotes.text: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; who God possesseth in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. H. W. Longfellow (d. 1882), public domain

quotes.text: At the close of life you will be examined as to your love.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. David Lewis (1864), public domain (his 'Spiritual Maxims', n. 70)

quotes.text: If you are what you ought to be, you will set fire to all Italy.
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Siena
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Vida D. Scudder (1905), public domain

quotes.text: I do not seek to understand that I may believe, but I believe in order to understand.
quotes.author: St. Anselm of Canterbury
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. S. N. Deane (1903), public domain

quotes.text: For He was made man that we might be made God.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Incarnation of the Word
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 4, public domain

quotes.text: The world was not conceived by chance and without reason, but for an useful end and for the great advantage of all beings, since it is really the school where reasonable souls exercise themselves, the training ground where they learn to know God.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Blomfield Jackson, NPNF series II, vol. 8, public domain

quotes.text: For that which He has not assumed He has not healed; but that which is united to His Godhead is also saved.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: Yesterday I was crucified with Him; today I am glorified with Him. Yesterday I died with Him; today I am quickened with Him.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: Christian, acknowledge thy dignity, and becoming a partner in the Divine nature, refuse to return to the old baseness by degenerate conduct.
quotes.author: St. Leo the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 12, public domain

quotes.text: In the distribution of alms and care of the poor, let our Christian fast-times be fat and abound; and let each bestow on the weak and destitute those dainties which he denies himself.
quotes.author: St. Leo the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. C. L. Feltoe, NPNF series II, vol. 12, public domain

quotes.text: The reason for loving God is God Himself; the measure, is to love Him without measure.
quotes.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: 19th-century English translation, public domain

quotes.text: For where can it be well with us without Him, and where ill if He be present?
quotes.author: St. Bernard of Clairvaux
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Sermons of St. Bernard on Advent and Christmas (St. Mary's Convent, 1909), public domain

quotes.text: If thou wouldst know how these things are done, ask grace, not learning; desire, not intellect; the groaning of prayer, not the diligence of reading; the spouse, not the master; God, not man.
quotes.author: St. Bonaventure
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Thomas Davidson (1887), public domain

quotes.text: He who prays is certainly saved; he who prays not is certainly damned.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: The Great Means of Salvation: Prayer
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. ed. E. Grimm (1886), public domain

quotes.text: Christ is the morning star, who promises and reveals to the saints the eternal light of life, when the night of the world is past.
quotes.author: St. Bede the Venerable
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. E. Marshall (1878), public domain

quotes.text: Prayer is an uprising of the mind to God or a petitioning of God for what is fitting.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 9, public domain

quotes.text: O Lord and Master of my life, give me not the spirit of sloth, of despondency, of ambition, or of idle talking; but bestow on me, Thy servant, the spirit of chastity, of humility, of patience, and of love.
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: traditional translation, public domain

quotes.text: Since then He Himself declared and said of the Bread, 'This is My Body,' who shall dare to doubt any longer?
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: We preach not one advent only of Christ, but a second also, far more glorious than the former.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 7, public domain

quotes.text: I am the wheat of God, and let me be ground by the teeth of the wild beasts, that I may be found the pure bread of Christ.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: For the glory of God is a living man; and the life of man consists in beholding God.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop, Martyr and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: He can no longer have God for his Father, who has not the Church for his mother.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.work: On the Unity of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 5, public domain

quotes.text: Eighty and six years have I served Him, and He never did me any injury: how then can I blaspheme my King and my Saviour?
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: And on the day called Sunday, all who live in cities or in the country gather together to one place, and the memoirs of the apostles or the writings of the prophets are read.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: What doth it profit thee to enter into deep discussion concerning the Holy Trinity, if thou lack humility, and be thus displeasing to the Trinity?
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Canon Regular
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. W. Benham (1874), public domain

quotes.text: Love is a great thing, a good above all others, which alone maketh every heavy burden light.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Canon Regular
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. W. Benham (1874), public domain

quotes.text: God has created me to do Him some definite service; He has committed some work to me which He has not committed to another. I have my mission.
quotes.author: St. John Henry Newman
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal
quotes.sourceEdition: Longmans (1893), public domain

quotes.text: Christ be with me, Christ within me, Christ behind me, Christ before me, Christ beside me, Christ to win me, Christ to comfort and restore me.
quotes.author: St. Patrick (attributed)
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. C. F. Alexander (1889), public domain

quotes.text: Hearken, O my son, to the precepts of thy master, and incline the ear of thine heart.
quotes.author: St. Benedict of Nursia
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot
quotes.sourceEdition: 19th-century English translation, public domain

quotes.text: The life of a monk ought at all times to have about it a Lenten character.
quotes.author: St. Benedict of Nursia
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot
quotes.sourceEdition: 19th-century English translation, public domain

quotes.text: Praised be my Lord God, with all His creatures, and especially our brother the Sun, who brings us the day, and who brings us the light.
quotes.author: St. Francis of Assisi
quotes.authorTitle: Founder of the Friars Minor
quotes.sourceEdition: 19th-century English translation, public domain

quotes.text: Take, Lord, and receive all my liberty, my memory, my intellect, and all my will—all that I have and possess.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola
quotes.authorTitle: Founder of the Society of Jesus
quotes.sourceEdition: trans. Elder Mullan, S.J. (1914), public domain

quotes.text: The Christian ideal has not been tried and found wanting. It has been found difficult; and left untried.
quotes.author: G. K. Chesterton
quotes.authorTitle: Author (not canonized)
quotes.sourceEdition: Cassell (1910), public domain

quotes.text: Love unites us to God; love covers a multitude of sins; love beareth all things, is long-suffering in all things.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: ANF vol. 1, public domain

quotes.text: Before the blessing of the heavenly words another nature is spoken of, after the consecration the Body is signified.
quotes.author: St. Ambrose of Milan
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF series II, vol. 10, public domain

quotes.text: There is one Physician who is possessed both of flesh and spirit; both made and not made; God existing in flesh; true life in death; both of Mary and of God; first passible and then impassible — even Jesus Christ our Lord.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Take heed, then, often to come together to give thanks to God, and show forth His praise. For when you assemble frequently in the same place, the powers of Satan are destroyed, and the destruction at which he aims is prevented by the unity of your faith. Nothing is more precious than peace, by which all war, both in heaven and earth, is brought to an end.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: It is better for a man to be silent and be [a Christian], than to talk and not to be one. It is good to teach, if he who speaks also acts. There is then one Teacher, who spoke and it was done; while even those things which He did in silence are worthy of the Father.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: For the beginning is faith, and the end is love. Now these two, being inseparably connected together, are of God, while all other things which are requisite for a holy life follow after them.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: All the pleasures of the world, and all the kingdoms of this earth, shall profit me nothing. It is better for me to die on behalf of Jesus Christ, than to reign over all the ends of the earth.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: My love has been crucified, and there is no fire in me desiring to be fed; but there is within me a water that lives and speaks, saying to me inwardly, Come to the Father. I have no delight in corruptible food, nor in the pleasures of this life. I desire the bread of God, the heavenly bread, the bread of life, which is the flesh of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: If, therefore, those who were brought up in the ancient order of things have come to the possession of a new hope, no longer observing the Sabbath, but living in the observance of the Lord's Day, on which also our life has sprung up again by Him and by His death — whom some deny, by which mystery we have obtained faith, and therefore endure, that we may be found the disciples of Jesus Christ, our only Master.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: See that you all follow the bishop, even as Jesus Christ does the Father, and the presbytery as you would the apostles; and reverence the deacons, as being the institution of God. Let no man do anything connected with the Church without the bishop. Wherever the bishop shall appear, there let the multitude [of the people] also be; even as, wherever Jesus Christ is, there is the Catholic Church.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: They abstain from the Eucharist and from prayer, because they confess not the Eucharist to be the flesh of our Saviour Jesus Christ, which suffered for our sins, and which the Father, of His goodness, raised up again.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: The priests indeed are good, but the High Priest is better; to whom the holy of holies has been committed, and who alone has been trusted with the secrets of God. He is the door of the Father, by which enter in Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, and the prophets, and the apostles, and the Church.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Let us look steadfastly to the blood of Christ, and see how precious that blood is to God which, having been shed for our salvation, has set the grace of repentance before the whole world.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: This is the way, beloved, in which we find our Saviour, even Jesus Christ, the High Priest of all our offerings, the defender and helper of our infirmity. By Him we look up to the heights of heaven. By Him we behold, as in a glass, His immaculate and most excellent visage. By Him are the eyes of our hearts opened. By Him our foolish and darkened understanding blossoms up anew towards His marvellous light.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Let our whole body, then, be preserved in Christ Jesus; and let every one be subject to his neighbour, according to the special gift bestowed upon him. Let the strong not despise the weak, and let the weak show respect unto the strong. Let the rich man provide for the wants of the poor; and let the poor man bless God, because He has given him one by whom his need may be supplied.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: How blessed and wonderful, beloved, are the gifts of God! Life in immortality, splendour in righteousness, truth in perfect confidence, faith in assurance, self-control in holiness! And all these fall under the cognizance of our understandings [now]; what then shall those things be which are prepared for such as wait for Him?
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Let us then continually persevere in our hope, and the earnest of our righteousness, which is Jesus Christ, who bore our sins in His own body on the tree, who did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth, but endured all things for us, that we might live in Him. Let us then be imitators of His patience; and if we suffer for His name's sake, let us glorify Him.
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: For whosoever does not confess that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh, is antichrist; and whosoever does not confess the testimony of the cross, is of the devil; and whosoever perverts the oracles of the Lord to his own lusts, and says that there is neither a resurrection nor a judgment, he is the first-born of Satan.
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: May the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, and Jesus Christ Himself, who is the Son of God, and our everlasting High Priest, build you up in faith and truth, and in all meekness, gentleness, patience, long-suffering, forbearance, and purity; and may He bestow on you a lot and portion among His saints, and on us with you, and on all that are under heaven, who shall believe in our Lord Jesus Christ.
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: What sober-minded man, then, will not acknowledge that we are not atheists, worshipping as we do the Maker of this universe, and declaring, as we have been taught, that He has no need of streams of blood and libations and incense; whom we praise to the utmost of our power by the exercise of prayer and thanksgiving for all things wherewith we are supplied.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Apologist and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: We who formerly delighted in fornication, but now embrace chastity alone; we who formerly used magical arts, dedicate ourselves to the good and unbegotten God; we who valued above all things the acquisition of wealth and possessions, now bring what we have into a common stock, and communicate to every one in need; we who hated and destroyed one another, and on account of their different manners would not live with men of a different tribe, now, since the coming of Christ, live familiarly with them, and pray for our enemies.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Apologist and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: We have been taught, and are convinced, and do believe, that He accepts those only who imitate the excellences which reside in Him, temperance, and justice, and philanthropy, and as many virtues as are peculiar to a God who is called by no proper name. And we have been taught that He in the beginning did of His goodness, for man's sake, create all things out of unformed matter.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Apologist and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: We have been taught that God did not make the world aimlessly, but for the sake of the human race; and we have before stated that He takes pleasure in those who imitate His properties, and is displeased with those that embrace what is worthless either in word or deed.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Apologist and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: And more than all other men are we your helpers and allies in promoting peace, seeing that we hold this view, that it is alike impossible for the wicked, the covetous, the conspirator, and for the virtuous, to escape the notice of God, and that each man goes to everlasting punishment or salvation according to the value of his actions.
quotes.author: St. Justin Martyr
quotes.authorTitle: Apologist and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: The Church, though dispersed throughout the whole world, even to the ends of the earth, has received from the apostles and their disciples this faith: [She believes] in one God, the Father Almighty, Maker of heaven, and earth, and the sea, and all things that are in them; and in one Christ Jesus, the Son of God, who became incarnate for our salvation; and in the Holy Spirit, who proclaimed through the prophets the dispensations of God.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: The Church, having received this preaching and this faith, although scattered throughout the whole world, yet, as if occupying but one house, carefully preserves it. She also believes these points [of doctrine] just as if she had but one soul, and one and the same heart, and she proclaims them, and teaches them, and hands them down, with perfect harmony, as if she possessed only one mouth.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: It is a matter of necessity that every Church should agree with this Church, on account of its preeminent authority [potiorem principalitatem].
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: For as the bread, which is produced from the earth, when it receives the invocation of God, is no longer common bread, but the Eucharist, consisting of two realities, earthly and heavenly; so also our bodies, when they receive the Eucharist, are no longer corruptible, having the hope of the resurrection to eternity.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: The Word of God, powerful in all things, and not defective with regard to His own justice, did righteously turn against that apostasy, and redeem from it His own property, not by violent means, as the [apostasy] had obtained dominion over us at the beginning, when it insatiably snatched away what was not its own, but by means of persuasion, as became a God of counsel, who does not use violent means to obtain what He desires.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: It is the conscience of the offerer that sanctifies the sacrifice when it is pure, and thus moves God to accept [the offering] as from a friend.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: As there are many rays of the sun, but one light; and many branches of a tree, but one strength based in its tenacious root; and since from one spring flow many streams, although the multiplicity seems diffused in the liberality of an overflowing abundance, yet the unity is still preserved in the source.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.work: On the Unity of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: The spouse of Christ cannot be adulterous; she is uncorrupted and pure. She knows one home; she guards with chaste modesty the sanctity of one couch. She keeps us for God. She appoints the sons whom she has born for the kingdom.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.work: On the Unity of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Let us therefore, brethren beloved, pray as God our Teacher has taught us. It is a loving and friendly prayer to beseech God with His own word, to come up to His ears in the prayer of Christ. Let the Father acknowledge the words of His Son when we make our prayer, and let Him also who dwells within in our breast Himself dwell in our voice.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Before all things, the Teacher of peace and the Master of unity would not have prayer to be made singly and individually, as for one who prays to pray for himself alone. For we say not My Father, which art in heaven, nor Give me this day my daily bread; nor does each one ask that only his own debt should be forgiven him. Our prayer is public and common; and when we pray, we pray not for one, but for the whole people, because we the whole people are one.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: The Father sent the Son to preserve us and give us life, in order that He might restore us; and the Son was willing to be sent and to become the Son of man, that He might make us sons of God; humbled Himself, that He might raise up the people who before were prostrate; was wounded that He might heal our wounds; served, that He might draw out to liberty those who were in bondage; underwent death, that He might set forth immortality to mortals.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: As water extinguishes fire, so almsgiving quenches sin. Here also it is shown and proved, that as in the layer of saving water the fire of Gehenna is extinguished, so by almsgiving and works of righteousness the flame of sins is subdued.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: She knows that she is but a sojourner on the earth, and that among strangers she naturally finds foes; and more than this, that her origin, her dwelling-place, her hope, her recompense, her honours, are above. One thing, meanwhile, she anxiously desires of earthly rulers — not to be condemned unknown.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Happy is our sacrament of water, in that, by washing away the sins of our early blindness, we are set free and admitted into eternal life! But we, little fishes, after the example of our ΙΧΘΥΣ Jesus Christ, are born in water, nor have we safety in any other way than by permanently abiding in water.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Nay, Lord, Your kingdom come with all speed — the prayer of Christians the confusion of the heathen, the exultation of angels, for the sake of which we suffer, nay, rather, for the sake of which we pray!
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: For the new grace of God has renewed all things from carnal unto spiritual, by superinducing the Gospel, the obliterator of the whole ancient bygone system; in which our Lord Jesus Christ has been approved as the Spirit of God, and the Word of God, and the Reason of God: the Spirit, by which He was mighty; the Word, by which He taught; the Reason, by which He came.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: All waters, therefore, in virtue of the pristine privilege of their origin, do, after invocation of God, attain the sacramental power of sanctification; for the Spirit immediately supervenes from the heavens, and rests over the waters, sanctifying them from Himself.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: There are two ways, one of life and one of death; but a great difference between the two ways. The way of life, then, is this: First, you shall love God who made you; second, your neighbour as yourself; and all things whatsoever you would should not occur to you, do not also do to another.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Neither pray as the hypocrites; but as the Lord commanded in His Gospel, thus pray: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be Your name. Your kingdom come. Your will be done, as in heaven, so on earth. Give us today our daily (needful) bread, and forgive us our debt as we also forgive our debtors. And bring us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one; for Yours is the power and the glory forever. Thrice in the day thus pray.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Even as this broken bread was scattered over the hills, and was gathered together and became one, so let Your Church be gathered together from the ends of the earth into Your kingdom; for Yours is the glory and the power through Jesus Christ forever.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: But every Lord's day gather yourselves together, and break bread, and give thanksgiving after having confessed your transgressions, that your sacrifice may be pure. But let no one that is at variance with his fellow come together with you, until they be reconciled, that your sacrifice may not be profaned.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: For the Christians are distinguished from other men neither by country, nor language, nor the customs which they observe. They dwell in their own countries, but simply as sojourners. As citizens, they share in all things with others, and yet endure all things as if foreigners. Every foreign land is to them as their native country, and every land of their birth as a land of strangers. They are in the flesh, but they do not live after the flesh. They pass their days on earth, but they are citizens of heaven.
quotes.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: To sum up all in one word — what the soul is in the body, Christians are in the world. The soul is dispersed through all the members of the body, and Christians are scattered through all the cities of the world. The soul dwells in the body, yet is not of the body; and Christians dwell in the world, yet are not of the world.
quotes.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: God Himself, who is almighty, the Creator of all things, and invisible, has sent from heaven, and placed among men, [Him who is] the truth, and the holy and incomprehensible Word. As a king sends his son, who is also a king, so sent He Him; as God He sent Him; as to men He sent Him; as a Saviour He sent Him, and as seeking to persuade, not to compel us; for violence has no place in the character of God.
quotes.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: O sweet exchange! O unsearchable operation! O benefits surpassing all expectation! That the wickedness of many should be hid in a single righteous One, and that the righteousness of One should justify many transgressors!
quotes.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: How beautiful is the spectacle to God when a Christian does battle with pain; when he is drawn up against threats, and punishments, and tortures; when, mocking the noise of death, he treads under foot the horror of the executioner; when he raises up his liberty against kings and princes, and yields to God alone, whose he is; when, triumphant and victorious, he tramples upon the very man who has pronounced sentence against him!
quotes.author: Minucius Felix
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 4, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Jesus is at all times assailed by false witnesses, and, while wickedness remains in the world, is ever exposed to accusation. And yet even now He continues silent before these things, and makes no audible answer, but places His defense in the lives of His genuine disciples, which are a pre-eminent testimony, and one that rises superior to all false witness, and refutes and overthrows all unfounded accusations and charges.
quotes.author: Origen of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 4, trans. Frederick Crombie (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: I do not congratulate that believer in Christ whose faith can be shaken by Celsus — who no longer shares the common life of men, but has long since departed — or by any apparent plausibility of argument.
quotes.author: Origen of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 4, trans. Frederick Crombie (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: 'Father,' said I, 'do you see, let us say, this vessel lying here to be a little pitcher, or something else?' And he said, 'I see it to be so.' And I replied to him, 'Can it be called by any other name than what it is?' And he said, 'No.' 'Neither can I call myself anything else than what I am, a Christian.'
quotes.author: St. Perpetua
quotes.authorTitle: Martyr
quotes.work: The Passion of the Holy Martyrs Perpetua and Felicity
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. R.E. Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: I saw a golden ladder of marvellous height, reaching up even to heaven, and very narrow, so that persons could only ascend it one by one; and on the sides of the ladder was fixed every kind of iron weapon. And under the ladder itself was crouching a dragon of wonderful size, who lay in wait for those who ascended, and frightened them from the ascent. And I said, 'In the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, he shall not hurt me.' And from under the ladder itself, as if in fear of me, he slowly lifted up his head; and as I trod upon the first step, I trod upon his head.
quotes.author: St. Perpetua
quotes.authorTitle: Martyr
quotes.work: The Passion of the Holy Martyrs Perpetua and Felicity
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. R.E. Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: And I comforted him, saying, 'On that scaffold whatever God wills shall happen. For know that we are not placed in our own power, but in that of God.' And he departed from me in sorrow.
quotes.author: St. Perpetua
quotes.authorTitle: Martyr
quotes.work: The Passion of the Holy Martyrs Perpetua and Felicity
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. R.E. Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Polycarp also was not only instructed by apostles, and conversed with many who had seen Christ, but was also, by apostles in Asia, appointed bishop of the Church in Smyrna, whom I also saw in my early youth, for he tarried [on earth] a very long time, and, when a very old man, gloriously and most nobly suffering martyrdom, departed this life, having always taught the things which he had learned from the apostles, and which the Church has handed down, and which alone are true.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: He has invented heresies and schisms, whereby he might subvert the faith, might corrupt the truth, might divide the unity. Those whom he cannot keep in the darkness of the old way, he circumvents and deceives by the error of a new way. He snatches men from the Church itself; and while they seem to themselves to have already approached to the light, and to have escaped the night of the world, he pours over them again, in their unconsciousness, new darkness.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.work: On the Unity of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: But be meek, since the meek shall inherit the earth. Be long-suffering and pitiful and guileless and gentle and good and always trembling at the words which you have heard. You shall not exalt yourself, nor give over-confidence to your soul. Your soul shall not be joined with lofty ones, but with just and lowly ones shall it have its intercourse. The workings that befall you receive as good, knowing that apart from God nothing comes to pass.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: As therefore the Lord did nothing without the Father, being united to Him, neither by Himself nor by the apostles, so neither do anything without the bishop and presbyters. Neither endeavour that anything appear reasonable and proper to yourselves apart; but being come together into the same place, let there be one prayer, one supplication, one mind, one hope, in love and in joy undefiled.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Keep yourselves from those evil plants which Jesus Christ does not tend, because they are not the planting of the Father. Not that I have found any division among you, but exceeding purity. For as many as are of God and of Jesus Christ are also with the bishop. And as many as shall, in the exercise of repentance, return into the unity of the Church, these, too, shall belong to God, that they may live according to Jesus Christ.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Christ is of those who are humble-minded, and not of those who exalt themselves over His flock. Our Lord Jesus Christ, the Sceptre of the majesty of God, did not come in the pomp of pride or arrogance, although He might have done so, but in a lowly condition, as the Holy Spirit had declared regarding Him.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Stand fast, therefore, in these things, and follow the example of the Lord, being firm and unchangeable in the faith, loving the brotherhood, and being attached to one another, joined together in the truth, exhibiting the meekness of the Lord in your intercourse with one another, and despising no one. When you can do good, defer it not, because alms delivers from death.
quotes.author: St. Polycarp of Smyrna
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: He who had wished to demonstrate to us, even in His own flesh, the flesh's infirmity, by the reality of suffering, said, 'Father, remove this Your cup'; and remembering Himself, added, 'save that not my will, but Yours be done.' He was the Will and the Power of the Father: and yet, for the demonstration of the patience which was due, He gave Himself up to the Father's Will.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: The oblation of the Church, therefore, which the Lord gave instructions to be offered throughout all the world, is accounted with God a pure sacrifice, and is acceptable to Him; not that He stands in need of a sacrifice from us, but that he who offers is himself glorified in what he does offer, if his gift be accepted. For by the gift both honour and affection are shown forth towards the King.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: God has loved mankind, on whose account He made the world, to whom He rendered subject all the things that are in it, to whom He gave reason and understanding, to whom alone He imparted the privilege of looking upwards to Himself, whom He formed after His own image, to whom He sent His only-begotten Son, to whom He has promised the kingdom in heaven, and will give it to those who have loved Him.
quotes.author: Author of the Epistle to Diognetus
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: But let our speech and petition when we pray be under discipline, observing quietness and modesty. Let us consider that we are standing in God's sight. We must please the divine eyes both with the habit of body and with the measure of voice. For God is the hearer, not of the voice, but of the heart.
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: These things therefore being manifest to us, and since we look into the depths of the divine knowledge, it behooves us to do all things in [their proper] order, which the Lord has commanded us to perform at stated times. He has enjoined offerings [to be presented] and service to be performed [to Him], and that not thoughtlessly or irregularly, but at the appointed times and hours. Where and by whom He desires these things to be done, He Himself has fixed by His own supreme will, in order that all things, being piously done according to His good pleasure, may be acceptable unto Him.
quotes.author: St. Clement of Rome
quotes.authorTitle: Pope and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: From Syria even unto Rome I fight with beasts, both by land and sea, both by night and day, being bound to ten leopards, I mean a band of soldiers, who, even when they receive benefits, show themselves all the worse. But I am the more instructed by their injuries [to act as a disciple of Christ]. Now I begin to be a disciple. And let no one, of things visible or invisible, envy me that I should attain to Jesus Christ.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Never at any time did Adam escape the hands of God, to whom the Father speaking, said, Let Us make man in Our image, after Our likeness. And for this reason in the last times, not by the will of the flesh, nor by the will of man, but by the good pleasure of the Father, His hands formed a living man, in order that Adam might be created [again] after the image and likeness of God.
quotes.author: St. Irenaeus of Lyons
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and William Rambaut (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Remember, Lord, Your Church, to deliver it from all evil and to make it perfect in Your love, and gather it from the four winds, sanctified for Your kingdom which You have prepared for it; for Yours is the power and the glory forever. Let grace come, and let this world pass away.
quotes.author: The Didache
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Document
quotes.work: The Didache (Teaching of the Twelve Apostles)
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 7, trans. M.B. Riddle (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: The outcry is that the State is filled with Christians — that they are in the fields, in the citadels, in the islands: they make lamentation, as for some calamity, that both sexes, every age and condition, even high rank, are passing over to the profession of the Christian faith; and yet for all, their minds are not awakened to the thought of some good they have failed to notice in it.
quotes.author: Tertullian
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Apologist
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 3, trans. S. Thelwall (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Paul, when enumerating the innumerable causes which generally separate men from the love of Christ and from the love of God in Christ Jesus, to all of which the love that was in himself rose superior, did not set down argument among the grounds of separation. For he says: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword? Nay, in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him that loved us.
quotes.author: Origen of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Early Christian Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 4, trans. Frederick Crombie (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: Now the virginity of Mary was hidden from the prince of this world, as was also her offspring, and the death of the Lord; three mysteries of renown, which were wrought in silence by God. How, then, was He manifested to the world? A star shone forth in heaven above all the other stars, the light of which was inexpressible, while its novelty struck men with astonishment.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Antioch
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 1, trans. Alexander Roberts and James Donaldson (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1885), public domain

quotes.text: But how great is the Lord's indulgence! How great His condescension and plenteousness of goodness towards us, seeing that He has wished us to pray in the sight of God in such a way as to call God Father, and to call ourselves sons of God, even as Christ is the Son of God — a name which none of us would dare to venture on in prayer, unless He Himself had allowed us thus to pray!
quotes.author: St. Cyprian of Carthage
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Ante-Nicene Fathers, Vol. 5, trans. Robert Ernest Wallis (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Such is its power, that it can raise us at once to heaven, if only we approach it with a sober mind.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homilies on the Gospel of John
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 14, trans. Philip Schaff (1889), public domain

quotes.text: It behooves therefore those who have been deemed worthy to see and to hear such things, and who have enjoyed so great a gift, to display also a life worthy of the doctrines, that they may enjoy also the good things which are (laid up) there.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homilies on the Gospel of John
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 14, trans. Philip Schaff (1889), public domain

quotes.text: For a dreadful, a dreadful thing is the love of money, it disables both eyes and ears, and makes men worse to deal with than a wild beast, allowing a man to consider neither conscience, nor friendship, nor fellowship, nor the salvation of his own soul.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homilies on the Gospel of John
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 14, trans. Philip Schaff (1889), public domain

quotes.text: He gave us Baptism, not that we should receive and depart, but that we should show the fruits of it in our after life.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 11, trans. J. Walker et al. (1889), public domain

quotes.text: For nothing is more frigid than a teacher who shows his philosophy only in words: this is to act the part not of a teacher, but of a hypocrite.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Homilies on the Acts of the Apostles
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 11, trans. J. Walker et al. (1889), public domain

quotes.text: If then we wish to enjoy pleasure, above all things else let us shun wickedness, and follow after virtue; since it is not in the nature of things for one to have a share thereof on any other terms, even if we were mounted upon the king's throne itself.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 11, trans. J. B. Morris (1889), public domain

quotes.text: There is nothing so good as thanksgiving; even as there is nothing worse than blasphemy.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 9, trans. W. R. W. Stephens (1889), public domain

quotes.text: One man inflamed with zeal is sufficient to reform a whole community!
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 9, trans. W. R. W. Stephens (1889), public domain

quotes.text: Poverty, to those who bear it wisely, is a great possession, a treasure that cannot be taken away; the stoutest of staves; a way of gain that cannot be thwarted; a lodging that is safe from snares.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 9, trans. W. R. W. Stephens (1889), public domain

quotes.text: The believer has his stand on the Rock; for this reason he cannot be overthrown by the dashing of the billows.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 9, trans. W. R. W. Stephens (1889), public domain

quotes.text: For all things in the eye of God are second to love.
quotes.author: St. John Chrysostom
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF First Series, Vol. 12, trans. T. W. Chambers (1889), public domain

quotes.text: Truth is always a quarry hard to hunt, and therefore we must look everywhere for its tracks.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: It follows that if there is any grace in the water, it is not of the nature of the water, but of the presence of the Spirit.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: Quiet, then, as I have said, is the first step in our sanctification; the tongue purified from the gossip of the world; the eyes unexcited by fair color or comely shape; the ear not relaxing the tone of mind by voluptuous songs, nor by that special mischief, the talk of light men and jesters.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: The study of inspired Scripture is the chief way of finding our duty, for in it we find both instruction about conduct and the lives of blessed men, delivered in writing, as some breathing images of godly living, for the imitation of their good works.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: The time which you lend to God is not lost: he will return it to you with large interest.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: Raise your soul above the earth; draw from its natural conformation the rule of your conduct; fix your conversation in heaven.
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Caesarea, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 8, trans. Blomfield Jackson (1895), public domain

quotes.text: For we ought to think of God even more often than we draw our breath; and if the expression is permissible, we ought to do nothing else.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: The Coming of God to Man, that we might go forth, or rather (for this is the more proper expression) that we might go back to God — that putting off the old man, we might put on the New; and that as we died in Adam, so we might live in Christ, being born with Christ and crucified with Him and buried with Him and rising with Him.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: This is a Feast of feasts and a Solemnity of solemnities as far exalted above all others (not only those which are merely human and creep on the ground, but even those which are of Christ Himself, and are celebrated in His honour) as the Sun is above the stars.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: God always was and always is, and always will be; or rather, God always Is.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: We needed an Incarnate God, a God put to death, that we might live.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Illumination is the greatest and most magnificent of the Gifts of God.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: The whole of our treatment and exertion is concerned with the hidden man of the heart, and our warfare is directed against that adversary and foe within us, who uses ourselves as his weapons against ourselves, and, most fearful of all, hands us over to the death of sin.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nazianzus
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Constantinople, Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Oration 2 (In Defence of His Flight to Pontus), 91
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Charles Gordon Browne and James Edward Swallow (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Thus there is in us the principle of all excellence, all virtue and wisdom, and every higher thing that we conceive: but pre-eminent among all is the fact that we are free from necessity, and not in bondage to any natural power, but have decision in our own power as we please; for virtue is a voluntary thing, subject to no dominion: that which is the result of compulsion and force cannot be virtue.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Nyssa, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 5, trans. H. A. Wilson (1893), public domain

quotes.text: For this reason man was brought into the world last after the creation, not being rejected to the last as worthless, but as one whom it behooved to be king over his subjects at his very birth.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Nyssa, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 5, trans. H. A. Wilson (1893), public domain

quotes.text: Love, therefore, is the foremost of all excellent achievements and the first of the commandments of the law.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Nyssa, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Soul and the Resurrection
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 5, trans. William Moore and Henry Austin Wilson (1893), public domain

quotes.text: We say that the Resurrection is the reconstitution of our nature in its original form.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Nyssa, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Soul and the Resurrection
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 5, trans. William Moore and Henry Austin Wilson (1893), public domain

quotes.text: The Creation proclaims outright the Creator; for the very heavens, as the Prophet says, declare the glory of God with their unutterable words.
quotes.author: St. Gregory of Nyssa
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop of Nyssa, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Soul and the Resurrection
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 5, trans. William Moore and Henry Austin Wilson (1893), public domain

quotes.text: For God is good, or rather is essentially the source of goodness: nor could one that is good be niggardly of anything: whence, grudging existence to none, He has made all things out of nothing by His own Word, Jesus Christ our Lord.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Incarnation of the Word
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 4, trans. Archibald Robertson (1892), public domain

quotes.text: For by the Word of God being made man has come about the destruction of death and the resurrection of life.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: On the Incarnation of the Word
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 4, trans. Archibald Robertson (1892), public domain

quotes.text: For if we too live as though dying daily, we shall not sin.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 4, trans. H. Ellershaw (1892), public domain

quotes.text: Wherefore virtue has need at our hands of willingness alone, since it is in us and is formed from us.
quotes.author: St. Athanasius
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 4, trans. H. Ellershaw (1892), public domain

quotes.text: As you have entered upon a good and most glorious path, run with reverence the race of godliness.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Edwin Hamilton Gifford (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Despise not the grace because it is freely given, but receive and treasure it devoutly.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Edwin Hamilton Gifford (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Faith is an eye that enlightens every conscience, and imparts understanding; for the Prophet says, And if you believe not, you shall not understand.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Edwin Hamilton Gifford (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Let us then not be ashamed of the Cross of our Saviour, but rather glory in it.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Edwin Hamilton Gifford (1894), public domain

quotes.text: Take therefore first, as an indestructible foundation, the Cross, and build upon it the other articles of the faith.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Jerusalem
quotes.authorTitle: Archbishop of Jerusalem, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 7, trans. Edwin Hamilton Gifford (1894), public domain

quotes.text: For thus we have been enriched by His poverty, the human nature having been brought up in Him to a God-befitting dignity, and established on thrones beyond all things.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Patriarch of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Paschal Letters or Paschal Homilies, trans. (Fathers-Synchronized-EN collection), public domain

quotes.text: For divinity and humanity have run together ineffably, and in a way that no one could either conceive or express, towards one thing henceforth conceived; so that He might be conceived at the same time as man like us, and God beyond us; thus both only-begotten and firstborn.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Patriarch of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Paschal Letters or Paschal Homilies, trans. (Fathers-Synchronized-EN collection), public domain

quotes.text: Thus we say that He is worshipped both by ourselves and by the holy angels, not unwisely bringing Him down to a mere humanity, but following the divine Scripture, and binding the Word who was begotten of God to our nature in a union, and weaving the product of both into a certain unity, so that He might not be considered as a mere man who bore God, but rather as God who became man.
quotes.author: St. Cyril of Alexandria
quotes.authorTitle: Patriarch of Alexandria, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Paschal Letters or Paschal Homilies, trans. (Fathers-Synchronized-EN collection), public domain

quotes.text: The Deity, therefore, is ineffable and incomprehensible.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: An Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 9, trans. S. D. F. Salmond (1899), public domain

quotes.text: He who longs always after God, he sees Him: for God is in all things.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: An Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 9, trans. S. D. F. Salmond (1899), public domain

quotes.text: And by thought it is that He creates, and thought is the basis of the work, the Word filling it and the Spirit perfecting it.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: An Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 9, trans. S. D. F. Salmond (1899), public domain

quotes.text: God therefore is both Creator and Provider, and His creative and preserving and providing power is simply His good-will.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: An Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 9, trans. S. D. F. Salmond (1899), public domain

quotes.text: Without faith it is impossible to be saved.
quotes.author: St. John of Damascus
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: An Exposition of the Orthodox Faith
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 9, trans. S. D. F. Salmond (1899), public domain

quotes.text: Love is a good disposition of the soul by which one prefers no being to the knowledge of God.
quotes.author: St. Maximus the Confessor
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. George C. Berthold, Paulist Press (1985); text also at saintandrewgoc.org

quotes.text: All the virtues assist the mind in the pursuit of Divine Love, but above all does pure prayer.
quotes.author: St. Maximus the Confessor
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. George C. Berthold, Paulist Press (1985); text also at saintandrewgoc.org

quotes.text: The one who loves God cannot help but love also every man as himself even though he is displeased by the passions of those who are not yet purified.
quotes.author: St. Maximus the Confessor
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. George C. Berthold, Paulist Press (1985); text also at saintandrewgoc.org

quotes.text: The one who sees a trace of hatred in his own heart through any fault at all toward any man whoever he may be makes himself completely foreign to the love of God, because love for God in no way admits hatred for man.
quotes.author: St. Maximus the Confessor
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. George C. Berthold, Paulist Press (1985); text also at saintandrewgoc.org

quotes.text: Blessed is the man who has learnt to love all men equally.
quotes.author: St. Maximus the Confessor
quotes.authorTitle: Abbot and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. George C. Berthold, Paulist Press (1985); text also at saintandrewgoc.org

quotes.text: To-day Godhead sealed itself upon Manhood, that so with the Godhead's stamp Manhood might be adorned.
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 13, trans. J. B. Morris and A. Edward Johnston (1898), public domain

quotes.text: To-day was born the Child, and His name was called Wonder! For a wonder it is that God as a Babe should show Himself.
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 13, trans. J. B. Morris and A. Edward Johnston (1898), public domain

quotes.text: In this night of reconcilement let no man be wroth or gloomy! in this night that stills all, none that threatens or disturbs!
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 13, trans. J. B. Morris and A. Edward Johnston (1898), public domain

quotes.text: The Babe that I carry carries me, says Mary, and He has lowered His wings, and taken and placed me between His pinions, and mounted into the air; and a promise has been given me that height and depth shall be my Son's.
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 13, trans. J. B. Morris and A. Edward Johnston (1898), public domain

quotes.text: Glory to the Hidden One, Whose Son was made manifest! Glory to that Living One, Whose Son was made to die! Glory to that Great One, Whose Son descended and was small!
quotes.author: St. Ephrem the Syrian
quotes.authorTitle: Deacon and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: NPNF Second Series, Vol. 13, trans. J. B. Morris and A. Edward Johnston (1898), public domain

quotes.text: Our life and our death are with our neighbour. If we gain our brother, we have gained our God; but if we scandalize our brother, we have sinned against Christ.
quotes.author: Abba Anthony the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Father of Monasticism
quotes.work: Sayings of the Desert Fathers (Apophthegmata Patrum)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Paradise of the Desert Fathers, EWTN Library (from Vitae Patrum tradition), public domain

quotes.text: Whenever we cover our brother's sin, God will cover ours; whenever we tell people about our brother's guilt, God will do the same about ours.
quotes.author: Abba Poemen
quotes.authorTitle: Desert Father
quotes.work: Sayings of the Desert Fathers (Apophthegmata Patrum)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Paradise of the Desert Fathers, EWTN Library (from Vitae Patrum tradition), public domain
