quotes.authorTitle: Servant of God and Catholic Worker founder
quotes.sourceEdition: Catholic Worker Press, 1948; cited in Wikiquote

quotes.text: I think that the Church is the only thing that is going to make the terrible world we are coming to endurable; the only thing that makes the Church endurable is that it is somehow the body of Christ and that on this we are fed.
quotes.author: Flannery O'Connor
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic author
quotes.work: The Habit of Being: Letters of Flannery O'Connor
quotes.sourceEdition: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 1979; quoted in The Marginalian (2014)

quotes.text: I write the way I do because (not though) I am a Catholic. This is a fact and nothing covers it like the bald statement.
quotes.author: Flannery O'Connor
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic author
quotes.work: The Habit of Being: Letters of Flannery O'Connor
quotes.sourceEdition: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 1979; quoted in The Marginalian (2014)

quotes.text: To the hard of hearing you shout, and for the almost-blind you draw large and startling figures.
quotes.author: Flannery O'Connor
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic author
quotes.locus: Essay: 'The Fiction Writer and His Country'
quotes.sourceEdition: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 1969; quoted in Word on Fire O'Connor Collection preview

quotes.text: The soul in which God dwells by grace is no impersonal scene of the divine life but is itself drawn into this life. The divine life is three-personal life: it is overflowing love, in which the Father generates the Son and gives him his Being, while the Son embraces this Being and returns it to the Father; it is the love in which the Father and Son are one, both breathing the Holy Spirit. By grace this Spirit is shed abroad in men's hearts.
quotes.author: St. Edith Stein (Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Martyr, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: ICS Publications; cited in Catholic Culture library review

quotes.text: The Christian mysteries are an indivisible whole. The way from Bethlehem leads inevitably to Golgotha, from the crib to the Cross. The star of Bethlehem shines in the night of sin. The shadow of the Cross falls on the light that shines from the crib. This light is extinguished in the darkness of Good Friday, but it rises all the more brilliantly in the sun of grace on the morning of the Resurrection.
quotes.author: St. Edith Stein (Teresa Benedicta of the Cross)
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Martyr, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Translated edition (Servant Books); cited in Catholic Culture library review

quotes.text: Our Lady said yes for us all. It was as if the human race were a little dark house, without light or air, locked and latched. The wind of the Spirit had beaten on the door, rattled the windows, tapped on the dark glass with the tiny hands of flowers… But one day, a girl opened the door, and the little house was swept pure and sweet by the wind.
quotes.author: Caryll Houselander
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic mystic and author
quotes.sourceEdition: Sheed & Ward, 1944; PDF via St. Mary's Cathedral

quotes.text: She is not only human; she is humanity. The one thing that she did and does is the one thing that we all have to do, namely, to bear Christ into the world.
quotes.author: Caryll Houselander
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic mystic and author
quotes.sourceEdition: Sheed & Ward, 1944; PDF via St. Mary's Cathedral

quotes.text: Virginity is really the whole offering of soul and body to be consumed in the fire of love and changed into the flame of its glory.
quotes.author: Caryll Houselander
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic mystic and author
quotes.sourceEdition: Sheed & Ward, 1944; PDF via St. Mary's Cathedral

quotes.text: The Catholic Church makes men. By which I do not mean boasters and swaggerers, nor bullies nor ignorant fools, who, finding themselves comfortable, think that their comfort will be a boon to others, and attempt (with singular unsuccess) to force it on the world; but men, human beings, different from the beasts, capable of firmness and discipline and recognition; accepting death; tenacious.
quotes.author: Hilaire Belloc
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic author and historian
quotes.sourceEdition: Project Gutenberg eBook #7373, public domain

quotes.text: She is Europe and all our past. She is returning.
quotes.author: Hilaire Belloc
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic author and historian
quotes.sourceEdition: Project Gutenberg eBook #7373, public domain

quotes.text: Be not afraid, it is but a pang now and then, and a struggle; a covenant with your eyes, and a fasting in the wilderness, some calm habitual watchfulness, and the hearty effort to obey, and all will be well. Be not afraid. He is most gracious, and will bring you on by little and little.
quotes.author: St. John Henry Newman
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal and Confessor
quotes.sourceEdition: Newman Reader edition (newmanreader.org), public domain

quotes.text: There are some who pass through life as through a tunnel, without ever understanding the splendour, the security and the warmth of the sun of faith.
quotes.author: St. Josemaría Escrivá
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder of Opus Dei
quotes.sourceEdition: escrivaworks.org, © Studium Foundation (authorised digital edition)

quotes.text: The family, which is founded and given life by love, is a community of persons: of husband and wife, of parents and children, of relatives. Its first task is to live with fidelity the reality of communion in a constant effort to develop an authentic community of persons.
quotes.author: St. John Paul II
quotes.authorTitle: Pope
quotes.sourceEdition: Vatican.va official English translation (1981)

quotes.text: Charity in truth, to which Jesus Christ bore witness by his earthly life and especially by his death and resurrection, is the principal driving force behind the authentic development of every person and of all humanity.
quotes.author: Pope Benedict XVI
quotes.authorTitle: Pope Emeritus
quotes.sourceEdition: Vatican.va official English translation (2009)

quotes.text: My children, the three acts of faith, hope and charity contain all the happiness of man upon the earth.
quotes.author: St. John Vianney
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Confessor (Curé d'Ars)
quotes.locus: Chapter 2: On the Love of God
quotes.sourceEdition: EWTN Library, Instructions on the Catechism (translation, public domain text)

quotes.text: Christianity is the only frame for pagan freedom.
quotes.author: G. K. Chesterton
quotes.authorTitle: Author and apologist
quotes.locus: Chapter IX: Authority and the Adventurer
quotes.sourceEdition: Project Gutenberg eBook #16769, public domain

quotes.text: Let humility be always at work, like the bee at the honeycomb, or all will be lost.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: I believe we shall never learn to know ourselves except by endeavouring to know God.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: Fix your eyes on the Crucified One, and all will seem easy.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: This is the end and aim of prayer, my daughters; this is the reason of the spiritual marriage whose children are always good works.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: Remember how important it is for you to have understood this truth—that the Lord is within us and that we should be there with Him.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Way of Perfection, trans. E. Allison Peers (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: The world is on fire. Men try to condemn Christ once again, as it were, for they bring a thousand false witnesses against Him. They would raze His Church to the ground—and are we to waste our time upon things which, if God were to grant them, would perhaps bring one soul less to Heaven?
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Way of Perfection, trans. E. Allison Peers (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: In order to arrive at having pleasure in everything, Desire to have pleasure in nothing. In order to arrive at possessing everything, Desire to possess nothing. In order to arrive at being everything, Desire to be nothing. In order to arrive at knowing everything, Desire to know nothing.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ascent of Mount Carmel, trans. E. Allison Peers, public domain

quotes.text: When thy mind dwells upon anything, Thou art ceasing to cast thyself upon the All. For, in order to pass from the all to the All, Thou hast to deny thyself wholly in all. And, when thou comest to possess it wholly, Thou must possess it without desiring anything. For, if thou wilt have anything in having all, Thou hast not thy treasure purely in God.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ascent of Mount Carmel, trans. E. Allison Peers, public domain

quotes.text: Oh, night that guided me, Oh, night more lovely than the dawn, Oh, night that joined Beloved with lover, Lover transformed in the Beloved!
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Dark Night of the Soul, trans. E. Allison Peers, 3rd rev. ed. (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: On this step the soul now walks so anxiously that it seeks the Beloved in all things. When it eats, when it sleeps, when it watches, when it does aught soever, all its care is about the Beloved.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Book II, Chapter XIX (Second Step of the Mystic Ladder)
quotes.sourceEdition: Dark Night of the Soul, trans. E. Allison Peers, 3rd rev. ed. (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: Where hast Thou hidden Thyself, And abandoned me to my sorrow, O my Beloved! Thou hast fled like the hart, Having wounded me. I ran after Thee, crying; but Thou wert gone.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Spiritual Canticle, trans. David Lewis, ed. Benedict Zimmerman, O.C.D. (1909), public domain

quotes.text: Seek Him in faith and love, without seeking to satisfy thyself in aught, or to understand more than is expedient for thee to know; for faith and love are the two guides of the blind; they will lead thee, by a way thou knowest not, to the secret chamber of God.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Spiritual Canticle, trans. David Lewis, ed. Benedict Zimmerman, O.C.D. (1909), public domain

quotes.text: Believe me, dear child, devotion is the sweetest of sweets, the queen of virtues, the perfection of love. If love is the milk of life, devotion is the cream thereof; if it is a fruitful plant, devotion is the blossom; if it is a precious stone, devotion is its brightness; if it is a precious balm, devotion is its perfume, even that sweet odour which delights men and causes the angels to rejoice.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: In short, devotion is simply a spiritual activity and liveliness by means of which Divine Love works in us, and causes us to work briskly and lovingly; and just as charity leads us to a general practice of all God's Commandments, so devotion leads us to practise them readily and diligently.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: Believe me, my daughter, there is no way to God save through this door.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Part II, Chapter I (On the Necessity of Prayer)
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: Prayer is nothing else but a conversation in which the soul amorously entertains herself with God concerning his most amiable goodness, to unite and join herself thereto.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Treatise on the Love of God
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on the Love of God, trans. Henry Benedict Mackey, O.S.B. (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: Reputation, after all, is but a signboard giving notice where virtue dwells, and virtue itself is always and everywhere preferable.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: The greatest glory we can give to God is to do his will in everything.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (EWTN edition), public domain

quotes.text: Conformity signifies that we join our wills to the will of God. Uniformity means more—it means that we make one will of God's will and ours, so that we will only what God wills; that God's will alone, is our will.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (EWTN edition), public domain

quotes.text: This is the summit of perfection and to it we should always aspire; this should be the goal of all our works, desires, meditations and prayers.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: Those who love God are always happy, because their whole happiness is to fulfill, even in adversity, the will of God.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (EWTN edition), public domain

quotes.text: The supreme wisdom is this, to work at the exclusion of all self-seeking, and to be ready to do the will of God.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: The whole world cannot afford thee peace, but the peace of God can.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: I am wont to visit My elect in two ways—by temptation and by consolation.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.locus: Book III, Chapter 3 (The Voice of Christ)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: Teach me, Lord, to do Your will. Teach me to live worthily and humbly in Your sight, for You are my wisdom Who know me truly, and Who knew me even before the world was made and before I was born into it.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.locus: Book III, Chapter 3 (Prayer for the Grace of Devotion)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: The world, which promises small and passing things, is served with great eagerness: I promise great and eternal things and the hearts of men grow dull.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.locus: Book III, Chapter 3 (The Voice of Christ)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: My God, I choose everything, I will not be a Saint by halves, I am not afraid of suffering for Thee, I only fear one thing, and that is to do my own will. Accept the offering of my will, for I choose all that Thou willest.
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Story of a Soul, trans. Thomas N. Taylor (CCEL/Gutenberg, 1922 ed.), public domain

quotes.text: Love attracts love; mine darts towards Thee, and would fain make the abyss brim over, but alas! it is not even as a dewdrop in the ocean. To love Thee as Thou lovest me, I must make Thy Love mine own. Thus alone can I find rest.
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Story of a Soul, trans. Thomas N. Taylor (CCEL/Gutenberg, 1922 ed.), public domain

quotes.text: I will spend my Heaven in doing good upon earth.
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Story of a Soul, trans. Thomas N. Taylor (CCEL/Gutenberg, 1922 ed.), public domain

quotes.text: I know and I have experienced that 'the Kingdom of God is within us.'
quotes.author: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Story of a Soul, trans. Thomas N. Taylor (CCEL/Gutenberg, 1922 ed.), public domain

quotes.text: Man is created to praise, reverence, and serve God our Lord, and by this means to save his soul.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder of the Society of Jesus
quotes.sourceEdition: The Spiritual Exercises, trans. Elder Mullan, S.J. (1914), public domain

quotes.text: For as strolling, walking and running are bodily exercises, so every way of preparing and disposing the soul to rid itself of all the disordered tendencies, and, after it is rid, to seek and find the Divine Will as to the management of one's life for the salvation of the soul, is called a Spiritual Exercise.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder of the Society of Jesus
quotes.sourceEdition: The Spiritual Exercises, trans. Elder Mullan, S.J. (1914), public domain

quotes.text: Also in this He shewed me a little thing, the quantity of an hazel-nut, in the palm of my hand; and it was as round as a ball. I looked thereupon with eye of my understanding, and thought: What may this be? And it was answered generally thus: It is all that is made. I marvelled how it might last, for methought it might suddenly have fallen to naught for little[ness]. And I was answered in my understanding: It lasteth, and ever shall [last] for that God loveth it. And so All-thing hath the Being by the love of God.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Anchoress
quotes.sourceEdition: Revelations of Divine Love, ed. Grace Warrack (1901), public domain

quotes.text: God, of Thy Goodness, give me Thyself: for Thou art enough to me, and I may nothing ask that is less that may be full worship to Thee; and if I ask anything that is less, ever me wanteth,—but only in Thee I have all.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Anchoress
quotes.sourceEdition: Revelations of Divine Love, ed. Grace Warrack (1901), public domain

quotes.text: It behoved that there should be sin; but all shall be well, and all shall be well, and all manner of thing shall be well.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Anchoress
quotes.sourceEdition: Revelations of Divine Love, ed. Grace Warrack (1901), public domain

quotes.text: Wouldst thou learn thy Lord's meaning in this thing? Learn it well: Love was His meaning. Who shewed it thee? Love. What shewed He thee? Love. Wherefore shewed it He? For Love. Hold thee therein and thou shalt learn and know more in the same. But thou shalt never know nor learn therein other thing without end. Thus was I learned that Love was our Lord's meaning.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Anchoress
quotes.sourceEdition: Revelations of Divine Love, ed. Grace Warrack (1901), public domain

quotes.text: All our perfection consists in being conformed, united, and consecrated to Jesus Christ; and therefore the most perfect of all devotions is, without any doubt, that which most perfectly conforms, unites and consecrates us to Jesus Christ.
quotes.author: St. Louis de Montfort
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder
quotes.work: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary
quotes.sourceEdition: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary, trans. Frederick William Faber (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: If, then, as is certain, the kingdom of Jesus Christ is to come into the world, it will be but a necessary consequence of the knowledge of the kingdom of the most holy Virgin Mary, who brought Him into the world the first time, and will make His second advent full of splendour.
quotes.author: St. Louis de Montfort
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder
quotes.work: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary
quotes.sourceEdition: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary, trans. Frederick William Faber (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: Great love can change small things into great ones, and it is only love which lends value to our actions. And the purer our love becomes, the less there will be within us for the flames of suffering to feed upon, and the suffering will cease to be a suffering for us; it will become a delight! By the grace of God, I have received such a disposition of heart that I am never so happy as when I suffer for Jesus, whom I love with every beat of my heart.
quotes.author: St. Faustina Kowalska
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Mystic
quotes.work: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul
quotes.sourceEdition: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul (Marian Press, 2005)

quotes.text: I am love and Mercy Itself. There is no misery that could be a match for My mercy, neither will misery exhaust it, because as it is being granted—it increases. The soul that trusts in My mercy is most fortunate, because I Myself take care of it.
quotes.author: St. Faustina Kowalska
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Mystic
quotes.work: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul
quotes.sourceEdition: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul (Marian Press, 2005)

quotes.text: All grace flows from mercy, and the last hour abounds with mercy for us. Let no one doubt concerning the goodness of God; even if a person's sins were as dark as night, God's mercy is stronger than our misery. One thing alone is necessary: that the sinner set ajar the door of his heart, be it ever so little, to let in a ray of God's merciful grace, and then God will do the rest.
quotes.author: St. Faustina Kowalska
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Mystic
quotes.work: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul
quotes.sourceEdition: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul (Marian Press, 2005)

quotes.text: No tongue can tell nor explain, no mind understand, the grievousness of Purgatory. But I, though I see that there is in Purgatory as much pain as in Hell, yet see the soul which has the least stain of imperfection accepting Purgatory, as I have said, as though it were a mercy, and holding its pains of no account as compared with the least stain which hinders a soul in its love.
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Genoa
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Tertiary
quotes.locus: Chapter: Of the necessity of Purgatory. How terrible it is.
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on Purgatory, trans. Charlotte Balfour and Helen Douglas Irvine (1858), public domain

quotes.text: The souls who are in Purgatory cannot, as I understand, choose but be there, and this is by God's ordinance who therein has done justly. They cannot turn their thoughts back to themselves, nor can they say, "Such sins I have committed for which I deserve to be here", nor, "I would that I had not committed them for then I would go now to Paradise."
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Genoa
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Tertiary
quotes.locus: Chapter: The state of the souls who are in Purgatory, how they are exempt from all self-love.
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on Purgatory, trans. Charlotte Balfour and Helen Douglas Irvine (1858), public domain

quotes.text: I believe no happiness can be found worthy to be compared with that of a soul in Purgatory except that of the saints in Paradise; and day by day this happiness grows as God flows into these souls, more and more as the hindrance to His entrance is consumed.
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Genoa
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Tertiary
quotes.locus: Chapter: On the happiness of souls in Purgatory
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on Purgatory, trans. Charlotte Balfour and Helen Douglas Irvine (1858), public domain

quotes.text: From this love-o'erflowing Passion, I take what fully makes up for the insufficiency of my own small merits; herein lies my complete justification; to meditate on this Passion, I call eternal wisdom, the perfection of all knowledge, the riches of all salvation, an entire satisfaction of all desert; it casts me down in prosperity, it raises me up in adversity, it keeps me in an even balance between this world's weal and woe, and guards me against all evil in complete security.
quotes.author: Bl. Henry Suso
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Priest and Mystic
quotes.sourceEdition: Little Book of Eternal Wisdom, trans. C. H. McKenna, O.P. (CCEL edition, 1910), public domain

quotes.text: Behold, assiduous meditation on My Passion makes out of a simple man a master of high knowledge; truly it is a living book in which everything is to be found. How right blessed is that man who has it ever before his eyes and studies it!
quotes.author: Bl. Henry Suso
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Priest and Mystic
quotes.sourceEdition: Little Book of Eternal Wisdom, trans. C. H. McKenna, O.P. (CCEL edition, 1910), public domain

quotes.text: The men who have gone beyond this, giving themselves up to God in the dying spiritual way and by renouncing all things, attaining to a state above the contemplation of the forms and images of the mind—in such men, let me assure you, children, God finds His place of rest; there He dwells and there He works as He wills.
quotes.author: Johannes Tauler
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Mystic
quotes.locus: Sermon for the First Sunday After the Epiphany: God is Gained by Detachment from Creatures
quotes.sourceEdition: The Sermons and Conferences of John Tauler, trans. Walter Elliott, C.S.P. (1910), public domain

quotes.text: When God thus encounters no resistance, He does His own will in the soul, drawing it to Himself and into Himself.
quotes.author: Johannes Tauler
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Mystic
quotes.locus: Sermon for the First Sunday After the Epiphany: God is Gained by Detachment from Creatures
quotes.sourceEdition: The Sermons and Conferences of John Tauler, trans. Walter Elliott, C.S.P. (1910), public domain

quotes.text: Let him never cease from prayer who has once begun it, be his life ever so wicked; for prayer is the way to amend it, and without prayer such amendment will be much more difficult.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Life of St. Teresa of Jesus, trans. David Lewis (1904), public domain

quotes.text: Love does not set them all to work suddenly, or equally, at all times and everywhere.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: God wills only our good; God loves us more than anybody else can or does love us.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (EWTN edition), public domain

quotes.text: Turn thee with thy whole heart unto the Lord, and forsake this wretched world, and thy soul shall find rest. Learn to despise outward things and to give thyself to things inward, and thou shalt see the kingdom of God come within thee.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: I remained, lost in oblivion; My face I reclined on the Beloved. All ceased and I abandoned myself, Leaving my cares forgotten among the lilies.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Dark Night of the Soul, trans. E. Allison Peers, 3rd rev. ed. (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: For to suffer without making excuses is a habit of great perfection, and very edifying and meritorious.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Way of Perfection, trans. E. Allison Peers (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: For this is the cause why we be not all in ease of heart and soul: that we seek here rest in those things that are so little, wherein is no rest, and know not our God that is All-mighty, All-wise, All-good. For He is the Very Rest.
quotes.author: Julian of Norwich
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Anchoress
quotes.sourceEdition: Revelations of Divine Love, ed. Grace Warrack (1901), public domain

quotes.text: A soul does not benefit as it should from the sacrament of confession if it is not humble. Pride keeps it in darkness. The soul neither knows how, nor is it willing, to probe with precision the depths of its own misery. It puts on a mask and avoids everything that might bring it recovery.
quotes.author: St. Faustina Kowalska
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Mystic
quotes.work: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul
quotes.sourceEdition: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul (Marian Press, 2005)

quotes.text: Whoever is desirous of great reward and everlasting salvation, of high knowledge and deep wisdom, of standing erect in joy and sorrow, of possessing full security against all evil, whoever wishes to drink a draught of Thy bitter Passion, and Thy singular sweetness, must carry Thee at all times, O crucified Jesus, before the eyes of his heart.
quotes.author: Bl. Henry Suso
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Priest and Mystic
quotes.sourceEdition: Little Book of Eternal Wisdom, trans. C. H. McKenna, O.P. (CCEL edition, 1910), public domain

quotes.text: After that we must surely say with the Saints, De Maria nunquam satis; we have not yet praised, exalted, honoured, loved, and served Mary as we ought to do.
quotes.author: St. Louis de Montfort
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder
quotes.work: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary
quotes.sourceEdition: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary, trans. Frederick William Faber (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: Strive always to prefer, not that which is easiest, but that which is most difficult; Not that which is most delectable, but that which is most unpleasing; Not that which gives most pleasure, but rather that which gives least; Not that which is restful, but that which is wearisome.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Ascent of Mount Carmel, trans. E. Allison Peers, public domain

quotes.text: Love desires secrecy; yea, though lovers may have nothing secret to say, yet they love to say it secretly.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Treatise on the Love of God
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on the Love of God, trans. Henry Benedict Mackey, O.S.B. (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: Though I am poor, Lord, I give thee all I possess; but when I say I give thee my will, I have nothing left to give thee.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: He who hath greater knowledge, and not the fear of God, is the more severe judge of himself.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: Therefore I maintain, my daughters, that we should fix our eyes on Christ our only good, and on His saints; there we shall learn true humility.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: No indeed, my child, the devotion which is true hinders nothing, but on the contrary it perfects everything; and that which runs counter to the rightful vocation of any one is, you may be sure, a spurious devotion.
quotes.author: St. Francis de Sales
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Introduction to the Devout Life, trans. John K. Ryan (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: The act and work of love—is strong as death, and emulation and importunity last as long as hell.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Book II, Chapter XIX (Fourth Step of the Mystic Ladder)
quotes.sourceEdition: Dark Night of the Soul, trans. E. Allison Peers, 3rd rev. ed. (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: When I see that the burden is beyond my strength, I do not consider or analyze it or probe into it, but I run like a child to the Heart of Jesus and say only one word to Him: 'You can do all things.' And then I keep silent, because I know that Jesus Himself will intervene in the matter, and as for me, instead of tormenting myself, I use that time to love Him.
quotes.author: St. Faustina Kowalska
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Mystic
quotes.work: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul
quotes.sourceEdition: Diary: Divine Mercy in My Soul (Marian Press, 2005)

quotes.text: Do not be afraid that the Lord Who has called us to drink of this spring will allow you to die of thirst.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Way of Perfection, trans. E. Allison Peers (CCEL edition), public domain

quotes.text: There is nothing more pleasing we can offer God than to say to him: 'Possess thyself of us.'
quotes.author: St. Louis de Montfort
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder
quotes.work: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary
quotes.locus: Part I, Chapter I, no. 1 (citing Alphonsus)
quotes.sourceEdition: True Devotion to the Blessed Virgin Mary, trans. Frederick William Faber (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: Perfection is founded entirely on the love of God: 'Charity is the bond of perfection;' and perfect love of God means the complete union of our will with God's.
quotes.author: St. Alphonsus Liguori
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Uniformity with God's Will, trans. Thomas W. Tobin, C.SS.R. (ecatholic2000 edition), public domain

quotes.text: Oh, what a sure path is the way of Thy Passion, along the way of truth, up to the very pinnacle of all perfection.
quotes.author: Bl. Henry Suso
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Priest and Mystic
quotes.sourceEdition: Little Book of Eternal Wisdom, trans. C. H. McKenna, O.P. (CCEL edition, 1910), public domain

quotes.text: I would fain send up a cry so loud that it would put fear in all men on the earth. I would say to them: 'Wretches, why do you let yourselves be thus blinded by the world, you whose need is so great and grievous, as you will know at the moment of death, and who make no provision for it whatsoever?'
quotes.author: St. Catherine of Genoa
quotes.authorTitle: Mystic and Tertiary
quotes.locus: Chapter: Reproaches which the souls in Purgatory make to people in the world
quotes.sourceEdition: Treatise on Purgatory, trans. Charlotte Balfour and Helen Douglas Irvine (1858), public domain

quotes.text: The sixth, to consider how He suffers all this for my sins, etc.; and what I ought to do and suffer for Him.
quotes.author: St. Ignatius of Loyola
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Founder of the Society of Jesus
quotes.locus: Third Week, First Day — First Contemplation, Sixth Point
quotes.sourceEdition: The Spiritual Exercises, trans. Elder Mullan, S.J. (1914), public domain

quotes.text: The glory of a good man is the testimony of a good conscience.
quotes.author: Thomas à Kempis
quotes.authorTitle: Augustinian Canon and Spiritual Writer
quotes.sourceEdition: The Imitation of Christ, trans. William Benham (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: Our Lord does not care so much for the importance of our works as for the love with which they are done.
quotes.author: St. Teresa of Ávila
quotes.authorTitle: Virgin and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: The Interior Castle, trans. Benedictines of Stanbrook, rev. ed. (1921), public domain

quotes.text: On this hungering step, the soul is nourished upon love; for, even as is its hunger, so is its abundance.
quotes.author: St. John of the Cross
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Book II, Chapter XIX (Fifth Step of the Mystic Ladder)
quotes.sourceEdition: Dark Night of the Soul, trans. E. Allison Peers, 3rd rev. ed. (CCEL), public domain

quotes.text: By no means then is it permitted to speak, or even to think, anything, concerning the superessential and hidden Deity, beyond those things divinely revealed to us in the sacred Oracles.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: This Good is celebrated by the sacred theologians, both as beautiful and as Beauty, and as Love, and as Beloved; and all the other Divine Names which beseem the beautifying and highly-favoured comeliness.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: And there is, further, the most Divine Knowledge of Almighty God, which is known, through not knowing (agnosia) during the union above mind; when the mind, having stood apart from all existing things, then having dismissed also itself, has been made one with the super-luminous rays, thence and there being illuminated by the unsearchable depth of wisdom.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: Now let us sing the Eternal Life, from which comes the self-existing Life, and every life; and from which, to all things however partaking of life, is distributed the power to live appropriately to each.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: Thou that instructeth Christians in Thy heavenly wisdom! Guide us to that topmost height of mystic lore which exceedeth light and more than exceedeth knowledge, where the simple, absolute, and unchangeable mysteries of heavenly Truth lie hidden in the dazzling obscurity of the secret Silence, outshining all brilliance with the intensity of their darkness.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. C. E. Rolt, Dionysius the Areopagite: On the Divine Names and the Mystical Theology (London: SPCK, 1920), public domain

quotes.text: Unto this Darkness which is beyond Light we pray that we may come, and may attain unto vision through the loss of sight and knowledge.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. C. E. Rolt, Dionysius the Areopagite: On the Divine Names and the Mystical Theology (London: SPCK, 1920), public domain

quotes.text: God is eternal; in this judgment all rational beings agree. Let us, then, consider what eternity is. For this word carries with it a revelation alike of the Divine nature and of the Divine knowledge. Now, eternity is the possession of endless life whole and perfect at a single moment.
quotes.author: St. Boethius
quotes.authorTitle: Philosopher and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. H. R. James (1897), Project Gutenberg, public domain

quotes.text: Why, then, ye children of mortality, seek ye from without that happiness whose seat is only within us?
quotes.author: St. Boethius
quotes.authorTitle: Philosopher and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. H. R. James (1897), Project Gutenberg, public domain

quotes.text: All things, then, seek the good; indeed, you may express the fact by defining good as that which all desire.
quotes.author: St. Boethius
quotes.authorTitle: Philosopher and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. H. R. James (1897), Project Gutenberg, public domain

quotes.text: Now every one will admit, that the 'Art of dying Well' is the most important of all sciences; at least every one who seriously reflects, how after death we shall have to give an account to God of every thing we did, spoke, or thought of, during our whole life, even of every idle word; and that the devil being our accuser, our conscience a witness, and God the Judge, a sentence of happiness or misery everlasting awaits us.
quotes.author: St. Robert Bellarmine
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Dalton (1847), public domain

quotes.text: This first great truth now remains established, that a good death depends upon a good life.
quotes.author: St. Robert Bellarmine
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Dalton (1847), public domain

quotes.text: For a most perfect mediator exercises the most perfect mediation possible in regard to some person for whom he mediates. Thus Christ exercised a most perfect act of mediation in regard to some person for whom He was Mediator. In regard to no person did He have a more exalted relationship than to Mary. Such, however, would not have been true had He not preserved Her from original sin.
quotes.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Friar Minor, Subtle Doctor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. from Balic ed.; repr. in The Catholic Library, catholiclibrary.org

quotes.text: It is a greater good to be preserved from evil than to fall into it and afterwards be freed from it. If Christ merited grace and glory for so many souls, who, for these gifts, are indebted to Christ as Mediator, why should no soul be His debtor for the gift of its innocence?
quotes.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Friar Minor, Subtle Doctor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. from Balic ed.; repr. in The Catholic Library, catholiclibrary.org

quotes.text: As soon as a man seriously seeks truth and goodness, this intimate conversation with himself tends to become conversation with God. Little by little, instead of seeking himself in everything, instead of tending more or less consciously to make himself a center, man tends to seek God in everything, and to substitute for egoism love of God and of souls in Him. This constitutes the interior life.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: The Three Ages of the Interior Life
quotes.locus: Vol. I, Part I, Chapter II: The Question of the One Thing Necessary
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. M. Timothea Doyle (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1947), public domain

quotes.text: The interior life, or the life of the soul with God, well deserves to be called the one thing necessary, since by it we tend to our last end and assure our salvation.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: The Three Ages of the Interior Life
quotes.locus: Vol. I, Part I, Chapter II: The Question of the One Thing Necessary
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. M. Timothea Doyle (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1947), public domain

quotes.text: Our whole life is thus shrouded in mystery. Every one of our salutary acts presupposes the mystery of grace from which it proceeds, and all sin is a mystery of iniquity, presupposing the divine permission to allow evil to exist in view of some higher good purpose, which will be clearly seen only in heaven.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Bede Rose, OSB (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1937); EWTN Library online

quotes.text: In the evening of our life, we shall be judged by love, namely, by the sincerity of our love for God, for our own soul, for our neighbor.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.locus: Chapter 8: The Grace of a Happy Death
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Cummins, OSB (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1952); EWTN Library online

quotes.text: Everyone who is wise according to God is blessed. The blessed life is the cognition of divinity. The cognition of divinity is the virtue of good work. The virtue of good work is the fruit of eternity.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: The first thing in the study of science is to seek God; then the honesty of life with the work of innocence.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: Prayer is of the heart, not of the lips. For God does not attend to the words of the supplicant, but looks upon the heart of the one praying.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: All progress proceeds from reading and meditation. For the things we do not know, we learn by reading; but the things we have learned, we preserve by meditations.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: The active life is the innocence of good works, the contemplative the speculation of supernal things; that one is common to many, this indeed to few.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: The highest perfection, therefore, of man in this life lies in this: that he is so united to God that his soul with all its powers and faculties becomes recollected in Him and is one spirit with Him.
quotes.author: St. Albert the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. attributed to a Benedictine of Princethorpe Priory (Project Gutenberg, 2011), public domain

quotes.text: This, I say, is the end of all perfection—that the soul may become so purified from every earthly longing, and so raised to spiritual things, that at last the whole life and the desires of the heart form one unbroken prayer.
quotes.author: St. Albert the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. attributed to a Benedictine of Princethorpe Priory (Project Gutenberg, 2011), public domain

quotes.text: He could not have commanded anything more beneficial, for this sacrament is the fruit of the tree of life. Anyone who receives this sacrament with the devotion of sincere faith will never taste death. It is a tree of life for those who grasp it, and blessed is he who holds it fast. The man who feeds on Me shall live on account of Me.
quotes.author: St. Albert the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. from Opera Omnia; repr. at crossroadsinitiative.com; public domain

quotes.text: Our God was not satisfied to be among us only by means of the inestimable mystery of the incarnation, by means of which God became man to allow mankind to share in his divine nature, but wanted to go further in an incomprehensible and infallible manner by means of the most divine sacrament of the altar in which truthfully speaking God is with us.
quotes.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Concerning the Most Holy Sacrament of the Altar
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Colbourne, OFM Cap; napcc.net; public domain

quotes.text: Among the most holy Sacraments of Christ's church the most excellent is the most divine Sacrament of the Eucharist, the Sacrament of Grace, the Sacrament of charity, the Sacrament of love.
quotes.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Concerning the Most Holy Sacrament of the Altar
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Colbourne, OFM Cap; napcc.net; public domain

quotes.text: A man is born natural and physical. In baptism he becomes supernatural and theological.
quotes.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Volume I, Lenten Sermon (Ash Wednesday season)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Vernon Wagner, OFM Cap, Opera Omnia; repr. at capuchins.org; public domain

quotes.text: There are two things, if I may speak summarily, which have been divinely handed down to us concerning the Virgin: that She was the Spouse of God and Mother of Christ.
quotes.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Mariale: On the Admirability of the Virgin Theotokos
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. from Opera Omnia S. Laurentii a Brundusio, Vol. 1 (Padua, 1964); capdox.capuchin.org.au; public domain

quotes.text: COME, then, let us extol the Peace Divine, and Source of conciliation, by hymns of peace! For this it is which unifies all, and engenders, and effects the agreement and fellowship of all.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: By the simplex and supernatural nature of all beautiful things, all beauty, and everything beautiful, pre-existed uniquely as to Cause.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: Whoso calm, serene, sedate, / Sets his foot on haughty fate; / Firm and steadfast, come what will, / Keeps his mien unconquered still; / Him the rage of furious seas, / Tossing high wild menaces, / Nor the flames from smoky forges / That Vesuvius disgorges, / Nor the bolt that from the sky / Smites the tower, can terrify.
quotes.author: St. Boethius
quotes.authorTitle: Philosopher and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. H. R. James (1897), Project Gutenberg, public domain

quotes.text: Mary had the greatest need of Christ as Redeemer, for she would have contracted original sin by reason of her descent unless this had been prevented by the grace of a Mediator. As others needed Christ that through His merit sin already contracted might be remitted to them, she needed even more a Mediator preventing this, that she might not at any time be under debt of contracting it, and that she be preserved from actually contracting it.
quotes.author: Bl. John Duns Scotus
quotes.authorTitle: Blessed, Friar Minor, Subtle Doctor
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. from Balic ed. (Oxon. XIV, 171); repr. in Wikisource, Duns Scotus, Defender of the Immaculate Conception

quotes.text: The primary object of contemplation is, in fact, God Himself and His infinite perfections, especially His goodness, His wisdom, and His providence.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Bede Rose, OSB (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1937); EWTN Library online

quotes.text: To attain heaven is to reach our ultimate and supernatural goal, to see God as He sees Himself, to love Him as He loves Himself, to possess Him unfailingly forever.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Cummins, OSB (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1952); EWTN Library online

quotes.text: He has reached the citadel of no small intelligence who knows that he cannot penetrate the secrets of God. Then, however, we rightly recognize God, when we deny that we know him perfectly.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: Of all the practices of the spiritual life purity of heart stands highest, and rightly, for it is the end and reward of all our labours, and is found only with those who live truly according to the spirit and as good religious.
quotes.author: St. Albert the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. attributed to a Benedictine of Princethorpe Priory (Project Gutenberg, 2011), public domain

quotes.text: For alms deliver from all sin and from death, and will not suffer the soul to go into darkness.
quotes.author: St. Robert Bellarmine
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Dalton (1847), public domain

quotes.text: They have given the preference to the ascent through negations, as lifting the soul out of things kindred to itself, and conducting it through all the Divine conceptions, above which towers that which is above every name, and every expression and knowledge, and at the furthest extremity attaching it to Him, as far indeed as is possible for us to be attached to that Being.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: The interior life of a just man who tends toward God and who already lives by Him is indeed the one thing necessary. To be a saint, neither intellectual culture nor great exterior activity is a requisite; it suffices that we live profoundly by God.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: The Three Ages of the Interior Life
quotes.locus: Vol. I, Part I, Chapter II
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. M. Timothea Doyle (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1947), public domain

quotes.text: He who is tormented by the passions of the soul, with the adversary lying in wait, should not therefore believe himself to be alienated from Christ because he suffers such things; but rather let him think that by this he is commendable to God, if, while he suffers these things, he praises God rather, not accuses.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text; public domain

quotes.text: Maker of earth and sky, from age to age / Who rul'st the world by reason; at whose word / Time issues from Eternity's abyss.
quotes.author: St. Boethius
quotes.authorTitle: Philosopher and Martyr
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. H. R. James (1897), Project Gutenberg, public domain

quotes.text: It is cause and origin and essence and life of all things; and even of those who fall away from It, both recalling and resurrection; and of those who have lapsed to the perversion of the Divine likeness, renewal and reformation; of those who are tossed about in a sort of irreligious unsteadiness, a religious stability; of those who have continued to stand, steadfastness.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Parker, M.A., Dionysius the Areopagite, Works (1897), public domain

quotes.text: Do you know why he is the temple of God? Because in him it pleased the Father that all fullness should dwell; in him the fullness of the Godhead dwelt corporately.
quotes.author: St. Lawrence of Brindisi
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Colbourne, OFM Cap; napcc.net; public domain

quotes.text: Charity can always grow greater in itself, because it is a participation in uncreated love and unlimited love.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.locus: Chapter 5: The Roots of Vice and Virtue
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Patrick Cummins, OSB (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1952); EWTN Library online

quotes.text: Goodness extends Its goodness by the very fact of Its existence unto all things.
quotes.author: (Pseudo-Dionysius)
quotes.authorTitle: Mystical Theologian and Doctor of the Church (attributed)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. C. E. Rolt, Dionysius the Areopagite: On the Divine Names and the Mystical Theology (London: SPCK, 1920), public domain

quotes.text: The entire separation of the soul from all earthly things and its close, unfettered union with God.
quotes.author: St. Albert the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. attributed to a Benedictine of Princethorpe Priory (Project Gutenberg, 2011), public domain

quotes.text: Enter ye in at the narrow gate: for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leadeth to destruction, and many there are who go in thereat. How narrow is the gate, and strait is the way that leadeth to life: and few there are that find it!
quotes.author: St. Robert Bellarmine
quotes.authorTitle: Cardinal, Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Rev. John Dalton (1847), public domain

quotes.text: More easily through fasting does prayer penetrate heaven.
quotes.author: St. Isidore of Seville
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. ReadAncientTexts.com, based on Latin text (facilius per ieiunium oratio penetrat caelum); public domain

quotes.text: To save our soul, one thing alone is necessary: to hear the word of God and to live by it. Therein lies the best part, which will not be taken away from a faithful soul even though it should lose everything else.
quotes.author: Cardinal Reginald Garrigou-Lagrange, OP
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: The Three Ages of the Interior Life
quotes.locus: Vol. I, Part I, Chapter II
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. M. Timothea Doyle (St. Louis: B. Herder, 1947), public domain

quotes.text: I assert, first, that the natural law is not only indicative of bad and good, but also contains its own proper prohibition of what is bad and prescription of what is good.
quotes.author: Francisco Suarez, SJ
quotes.authorTitle: Jesuit Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: De Legibus ac Deo Legislatore (On Laws and God the Lawgiver)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Alfred J. Freddoso, De Legibus 2, chap. 6 (Notre Dame course materials, public domain)

quotes.text: For, absolutely speaking, God was able to prescribe or prohibit nothing at all, and yet on the supposition that he has willed to have subjects with the use of reason, he was unable not to be their lawgiver, at least in those matters that are necessary for natural moral uprightness.
quotes.author: Francisco Suarez, SJ
quotes.authorTitle: Jesuit Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: De Legibus ac Deo Legislatore (On Laws and God the Lawgiver)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Alfred J. Freddoso, De Legibus 2, chap. 6 (Notre Dame course materials, public domain)

quotes.text: Therefore, the natural law includes God's will, because without his will there is no legislation. Then, too, the obligation that pertains to the natural law is a true obligation.
quotes.author: Francisco Suarez, SJ
quotes.authorTitle: Jesuit Priest and Theologian
quotes.work: De Legibus ac Deo Legislatore (On Laws and God the Lawgiver)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Alfred J. Freddoso, De Legibus 2, chap. 6 (Notre Dame course materials, public domain)

quotes.text: An understanding of this doctrine is so necessary that without it no one can study metaphysics, and ignorance of it gives rise to many errors in other sciences.
quotes.author: Thomas Cajetan (Cardinal de Vio)
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Cardinal, Thomistic Commentator
quotes.work: De Nominum Analogia (The Analogy of Names)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Edward A. Bushinski and Henry J. Koren, The Analogy of Names and the Concept of Being (Pittsburgh: Duquesne UP, 1953); full text at Archive.org, public domain

quotes.text: Almost all analogous names first were univocal and then by extension were rendered analogous, i.e. common by proportion to those things in which they are univocal and to others or to another. For example, the name wisdom was at first given to human wisdom and was univocal to the wisdoms of all men. Then, when men rose to a knowledge of the divine nature and saw the proportional similitude between us insofar as we are wise and God, they extended the name wisdom to signify in God that to which our wisdom is proportional.
quotes.author: Thomas Cajetan (Cardinal de Vio)
quotes.authorTitle: Dominican Cardinal, Thomistic Commentator
quotes.work: De Nominum Analogia (The Analogy of Names)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Edward A. Bushinski and Henry J. Koren, The Analogy of Names and the Concept of Being (Pittsburgh: Duquesne UP, 1953); full text at Archive.org, public domain

quotes.text: We are speaking of God; what marvel, if thou do not comprehend? For if thou comprehend, He is not God. Be there a pious confession of ignorance, rather than a rash profession of knowledge. To reach to God in any measure by the mind, is a great blessedness; but to comprehend Him, is altogether impossible.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Sermon 117 (On the Words of the Gospel of John 1:1-3, Against the Arians)
quotes.sourceEdition: R.G. MacMullen trans., Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, First Series, Vol. 6, ed. Philip Schaff (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1888), public domain; Latin in Patrologia Latina 38, 663

quotes.text: the New Testament lies hidden in the Old and the Old Testament is unveiled in the New.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Book 2, Q. 73; PL 34, 623; cited in Catechism of the Catholic Church §129
quotes.sourceEdition: Catechism of the Catholic Church §129, Libreria Editrice Vaticana, public domain; citing Quaest. in Hept. 2,73: PL 34,623; cf. DV 16

quotes.text: What the soul is to the human body, the Holy Spirit is to the Body of Christ, which is the Church.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: Sermo 267, 4; PL 38, 1231D; cited in Catechism of the Catholic Church §797
quotes.sourceEdition: Catechism of the Catholic Church §797, Libreria Editrice Vaticana, public domain; citing Sermo 267,4:PL 38,1231D

quotes.text: For our life in this sojourning cannot be without temptation: because our advance is made through our temptation, nor does a man become known to himself unless tempted, nor can he be crowned except he shall have conquered, nor can he conquer except he shall have striven, nor can he strive except he shall have experienced an enemy, and temptations.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Expositions on the Psalms (Enarrationes in Psalmos)
quotes.locus: Exposition on Psalm 60 (61 in Hebrew), section 2–3
quotes.sourceEdition: A Cleveland Coxe trans., Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, First Series, Vol. 8, ed. Philip Schaff (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1888), public domain

quotes.text: No one there hears Him calling, Come unto me all you that labour. They scorn to learn of Him, because He is meek and lowly of heart; for You have hid those things from the wise and prudent, and hast revealed them unto babes.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: J.G. Pilkington trans., Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, First Series, Vol. 1, ed. Philip Schaff (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: For faith is to believe what thou yet seest not; and the reward of faith is, to see what thou believest.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: Sermons on the New Testament (Sermones de Scripturis)
quotes.sourceEdition: R.G. MacMullen trans., Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, First Series, Vol. 6, ed. Philip Schaff (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1888), public domain

quotes.text: In that way the first part is humility; the second, humility; the third, humility: and this I would continue to repeat as often as you might ask direction, not that there are no other instructions which may be given, but because, unless humility precede, accompany, and follow every good action which we perform, being at once the object which we keep before our eyes, the support to which we cling, and the monitor by which we are restrained, pride wrests wholly from our hand any good work on which we are congratulating ourselves.
quotes.author: St. Augustine of Hippo
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.sourceEdition: J.G. Cunningham trans., Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers, First Series, Vol. 1, ed. Philip Schaff (Buffalo, NY: Christian Literature Publishing Co., 1886), public domain

quotes.text: Believing is an act of the intellect assenting to the Divine truth at the command of the will moved by the grace of God.
quotes.author: St. Thomas Aquinas
quotes.authorTitle: Priest and Doctor of the Church
quotes.locus: II-II, q. 2, a. 9, respondeo; cited in Catechism of the Catholic Church §155
quotes.sourceEdition: Dominican Province of England trans. ("Benziger Bros." edition, 1947), public domain; cited in CCC §155, Libreria Editrice Vaticana

quotes.text: You cannot love a thing without wanting to fight for it. You cannot fight without something to fight for. To love a thing without wishing to fight for it is not love at all; it is lust.
quotes.author: G. K. Chesterton
quotes.authorTitle: Catholic Author and Apologist
quotes.work: Appreciations and Criticisms of the Works of Charles Dickens
quotes.sourceEdition: G.K. Chesterton, Appreciations and Criticisms of the Works of Charles Dickens (London: J.M. Dent & Sons, 1911), public domain

quotes.text: The people most tempted are those who are ready, with the grace of God, to sacrifice everything for the salvation of their poor souls, who renounce all those things which most people eagerly seek. It is not one devil only who tempts them, but millions seek to entrap them.
quotes.author: St. John Vianney
quotes.authorTitle: Priest, Confessor of Ars
quotes.work: Sermons of the Curé of Ars
quotes.locus: Sermon 5: "We Are Nothing In Ourselves" (on Temptation)
quotes.sourceEdition: The Sermons of Saint John Mary Vianney (trans. Una Morrissy, 1960); also available at eCatholic2000, public domain

quotes.text: If you hoard it, you won't keep it; if you scatter, you won't lose. For (says the scripture), 'He has dispersed, he has given to the poor; his righteousness endures forever.'
quotes.author: St. Basil the Great
quotes.authorTitle: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
quotes.work: To the Rich (Ὁμιλία πρὸς τοὺς πλουτοῦντας / Homilia in divites)
quotes.sourceEdition: Trans. Peter Gilbert, from the Greek text in Patrologia Graeca, vol. 31 (Migne), public domain; cf. C. Paul Schroeder trans., On Social Justice (St. Vladimir's Seminary Press, 2009)
